summary: Extracts Fernet and credential rotation shell scripts from inline Go string constants into standalone .sh files under scripts/, embeds them via go:embed, and mounts them into CronJob pods as immutable, content-hash-named ConfigMaps. Adds a Makefile shellcheck target, extends CI shellcheck coverage, adds embedded content tests, and comprehensively updates the keystone reconciler reference documentation.

tests_checklist.item: All existing unit tests pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... — 0 failures)
Fernet CronJob spec tests updated: Command, volume count (4→5), mount count (3→4), scripts volume assertions
Credential CronJob spec tests updated: Command, volume count (4→5), mount count (3→4), scripts volume assertions
TestFernetRotateScript_EmbeddedContent verifies non-empty, shebang, set -e, fernet_rotate, python3 heredoc, error handling
TestCredentialRotateScript_EmbeddedContent verifies non-empty, shebang, set -e, credential_rotate+migrate, python3 heredoc, error handling
Script ConfigMap creation verified in SecretAlreadyExists tests (both fernet and credential)
SecurityContext tests updated to pass scriptConfigMapName parameter
go vet passes with no errors

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing CreateImmutableConfigMap pattern from reconcile_config.go
Fernet and credential implementations are symmetric (identical structure)
go:embed replaces inline constants cleanly — no residual dead code
Removed //nolint:gosec G101 comment (no longer needed for go:embed var)
Error wrapping follows existing fmt.Errorf pattern with context
Step numbering in comments updated consistently (3→6 steps)

security_checklist.item: restrictedSecurityContext() still applied to all init and main containers (no regression)
ConfigMap DefaultMode 0555 (r-xr-xr-x) — executable but not writable
Scripts use ssl.create_default_context with CA cert (proper TLS verification)
No secrets passed as command-line arguments

architecture_checklist.item: Uses config.CreateImmutableConfigMap (shared library, not raw corev1.ConfigMap)
Content-hash naming ensures CronJob spec updates when script changes
CronJob spec diff shows ONLY: added scripts volume+mount, changed Command
All other CronJob fields unchanged (env, image, schedule, RBAC, security context)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code between fernet and credential (symmetric but each handles its own type)
No unnecessary abstractions — direct go:embed + CreateImmutableConfigMap

fail_fast_checklist.item: Script ConfigMap creation error returned immediately with context
Broken go:embed produces empty string — caught by TestXxxScript_EmbeddedContent tests

defensive_checklist.item: Scripts have set -e for immediate failure on command errors
Python heredoc checks HTTP response status >= 300
Embedded content tests guard against silent go:embed breakage

issues_found.description: Missing SPDX Apache-2.0 license headers on new shell scripts. Per mandatory pattern 'SPDX Apache-2.0 header on infrastructure files' (applies_when: 'Adding any new shell script to the repository'), all .sh files must have the SPDX header after the shebang. Both fernet_rotate.sh and credential_rotate.sh start with '#!/bin/sh\nset -e' without the required 3-line SPDX block.
issues_found.fix:
Add SPDX header lines 2-4 after the shebang in both files:
#!/bin/sh
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright 2026 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
set -e
...
issues_found.evidence: fernet_rotate.sh line 1-2: '#!/bin/sh\nset -e' — missing SPDX block. Compare with hack/ci-build-service-image.sh which has SPDX on lines 2-4 per pattern.

issues_found.description: Task 4.2 specifies creating docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md as a standalone reference document. This file does not exist. The documentation content was instead integrated into docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md, which covers the go:embed pattern, ConfigMap mounting, content-hash naming, and error handling. The content is comprehensive and arguably better-placed, but deviates from the explicit task specification.
issues_found.fix: Either: (a) create the standalone docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md per task spec with a focused explanation of how to modify rotation scripts and run shellcheck locally, or (b) explicitly document in the progress JSON that task 4.2 was satisfied by updating keystone-reconciler.md instead.
issues_found.evidence: ls docs/reference/backend/ returns only: helm-values-schema.md, keystone-operator-packaging.md — no rotation-scripts.md. Task 4.2 description: 'Create docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md documenting: (1) the go:embed pattern, (2) ConfigMap mounting, (3) how to modify rotation scripts, (4) how to run shellcheck locally.'

suggested_improvements: CI shellcheck step (.github/workflows/ci.yaml:173) duplicates the Makefile shellcheck target logic. Consider having CI run 'make shellcheck' to maintain a single source of truth for which script globs are linted, per 'eliminate duplicated source-of-truth between CI and build system' review pattern.

The keystone-reconciler.md documentation update also corrects pre-existing inaccuracies (hardcoded 'keystone-' prefixes → '{name}-' parameterization, diagram reordering to match actual code). These corrections are accurate and improve doc quality, but are tangential to CC-0073 scope — consider noting them in the commit message for traceability.
next_steps: Add SPDX Apache-2.0 headers to both fernet_rotate.sh and credential_rotate.sh (AUTO_FIX)
Decide on docs/reference/backend/rotation-scripts.md: create standalone file or document deviation from task 4.2
Update embedded content tests if SPDX headers change the script prefix (shebang line test should still pass)

detected_patterns.name: Embedded script via go:embed with versioned ConfigMap mount
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding a new shell script that needs to run inside a CronJob or Job pod managed by an operator reconciler, where the script content should be independently lintable, testable, and versioned
detected_patterns.description: Shell scripts are stored as standalone .sh files under a scripts/ subdirectory co-located with the reconciler that uses them. The script is embedded into the Go binary via '//go:embed scripts/<name>.sh' as a string variable. At reconciliation time, the script content is passed to config.CreateImmutableConfigMap() which creates an immutable ConfigMap with a content-hash suffix (e.g., '{name}-fernet-rotate-script-abc123'). The ConfigMap is mounted as a volume with DefaultMode 0555 (executable, read-only) at /scripts/ in the pod spec. The container Command references the mounted path (e.g., ['/scripts/fernet_rotate.sh']) instead of inline 'sh -c <script>'. A companion unit test (TestXxxScript_EmbeddedContent) verifies the embedded variable is non-empty, starts with the correct shebang, and contains key commands — guarding against broken go:embed directives.
